mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC > 0.05 when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration and weak consumer spending, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of cases during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust employment growth and sustained wage income, supporting near-term consumer spending and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases but 5-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 325.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 325.0 but 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real personal consumption expenditures grow faster than real GDP signals consumption-led economic expansion, which disproportionately benefits equity markets and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth outpaces GDPC1 growth but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals immediate supply-side contraction and weak business activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops > 0.5% monthly but 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances.
